title: Getting started with enphaseR
text: need three credentials, free: Go https://developer-v4.enphase.com create developer account (separate homeowner/Enlighten account). Choose free Watt plan (1,000 API requests per month, 10 per minute — plenty daily fetch). Create application, grant access System Details Site Level Production Monitoring asked application can use. matters: Enphase checks endpoint application’s access list, application missing category gets 401 authorized access resource endpoints even valid credentials. enphase_systems() enphase_summary() need System Details; enphase_production() needs Site Level Production Monitoring. application’s page shows three values need: Client ID, Client Secret, API Key.

title: Getting started with enphaseR
text: interactive R session: browser opens Enphase’s authorization page. Log homeowner (Enlighten) account — one use Enphase app — approve access, copy code displays, paste R console. ’s . Credentials tokens saved (restrictive permissions) tools::R_user_dir("enphaseR", "config"), wherever ENPHASER_CONFIG_DIR environment variable config_dir argument points.
code: library(enphaseR)  enphase_authorize(   client_id = "your-client-id",   client_secret = "your-client-secret",   api_key = "your-api-key" )

title: Getting started with enphaseR
text: homeowners one row; note system_id.
code: enphase_systems()

title: Getting started with enphaseR
text: enphase_production() returns tibble one row per 15-minute interval: date, interval’s end time (end_at), watt-hours delivered (wh_del). completed day 96 intervals. Enphase hasn’t finished reporting recent day ’ll get fewer rows warning; just re-fetch day later. Don’t expect totals match Enlighten app exactly. Enphase records production two ways — site’s revenue-grade production meter microinverters’ reports — two typically differ around 1% measure different points system. enphase_production() returns meter values; app may display either source. mismatch size isn’t bug anyone’s part. Saving — result ordinary tibble, use whatever format like: ’s also enphase_summary(system_id) one-row snapshot current power, today’s energy, lifetime production.
code: # Yesterday (the default): prod <- enphase_production(system_id = 1234567)  # A specific day: enphase_production(1234567, start_date = "2026-06-15")  # A range -- one API request per day, automatically paced to respect # the free plan's rate limit: june <- enphase_production(   1234567,   start_date = "2026-06-01",   end_date = "2026-06-30" ) readr::write_csv(prod, "production.csv") # or saveRDS(prod, "production.rds"), arrow::write_parquet(), ...

title: Getting started with enphaseR
text: whole point: Schedule small script runs daily. Every run refreshes rotates tokens side effect, keeps authentication alive indefinitely. complete example script, fetch_solar.R, appends yesterday’s data CSV:
code: library(enphaseR)  system_id <- 1234567 csv_path <- "~/solar/production.csv"  new_rows <- enphase_production(system_id)  if (file.exists(csv_path)) {   old <- readr::read_csv(csv_path, show_col_types = FALSE)   # Keep only intervals we don't already have   new_rows <- dplyr::anti_join(new_rows, old, by = c("date", "end_at"))   readr::write_csv(new_rows, csv_path, append = TRUE) } else {   readr::write_csv(new_rows, csv_path) }  message(nrow(new_rows), " new rows written")

title: Getting started with enphaseR
text: Run every morning 6:05 (production yesterday complete ): Add entry crontab -e. cron runs different user one ran enphase_authorize(), set ENPHASER_CONFIG_DIR script (pass config_dir=) finds token files.
code: 5 6 * * * /usr/bin/Rscript /home/you/solar/fetch_solar.R >> /home/you/solar/fetch.log 2>&1

title: Getting started with enphaseR
text: Either use Task Scheduler directly — create daily task whose action runs Rscript.exe C:\path\\fetch_solar.R — set R taskscheduleR package: Note scheduled task runs machine ; computer sleeps scheduled time, enable “Run task soon possible scheduled start missed” task’s settings.
code: taskscheduleR::taskscheduler_create(   taskname = "enphase_daily",   rscript = "C:/path/to/fetch_solar.R",   schedule = "DAILY",   starttime = "06:05" )

title: Getting started with enphaseR
text: silent cron job can fail month without noticing — exactly refresh token lapses. script log file (cron line ) , ideally, alert failure. minimal pattern:
code: result <- try(enphase_production(system_id)) if (inherits(result, "try-error")) {   # write to a log, send an email (e.g. with the emayili package),   # or trigger any notification you already use }

what: If the refresh token lapses anyway
title: Getting started with enphaseR
text: ’ll see error like: Recovery one -argument call interactive session — saved credentials reused, ’s nothing look : scheduled job resumes working, past days can backfilled start_date/end_date range.
code: Enphase rejected the token refresh. The refresh token has likely expired (they last about a month) — run enphase_authorize() to re-authorize in the browser, then try again. enphase_authorize()

code: @Manual{,   title = {enphaseR: Access Solar Production Data from the Enphase Enlighten Cloud API},   author = {Sharon Machlis},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://smach.github.io/enphaseR/}, }

what: Access Solar Production Data from the Enphase Enlighten Cloud API
title: Access Solar Production Data from the Enphase Enlighten Cloud API
text: Fetch solar production data Enphase system official Enphase Enlighten cloud API (v4), R. hard part Enphase API authentication, ’s package handles: one-time browser authorization, enphaseR refreshes rotates OAuth tokens every API call. scheduled job runs least monthly (daily typical) keeps authentication alive indefinitely — never log . Two limitations built Enphase’s API worth knowing front: first authorization requires browser login; enphase_authorize() walks . Refresh tokens last month. nothing calls API longer (machine , job disabled), repeat browser step — single -argument enphase_authorize() call. Note: package talks cloud API, works anywhere covers historical data. want live readings Envoy gateway local network, see microinverterdata package instead.

title: Access Solar Production Data from the Enphase Enlighten Cloud API
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("smach/enphaseR")  # or from a local copy of this directory: # install.packages("path/to/enphaseR", repos = NULL, type = "source")

title: Access Solar Production Data from the Enphase Enlighten Cloud API
text: Create free developer account application https://developer-v4.enphase.com (free Watt plan allows 1,000 requests/month — plenty daily fetch). creating application, grant access “System Details” “Site Level Production Monitoring.” System Details allows listing systems reading summaries; Site Level Production Monitoring covers production intervals. application missing one gets 401 authorized access resource affected endpoints even though credentials perfectly valid — hint app’s access list problem. application page shows three values need: Client ID, Client Secret, API Key. interactive R session: browser opens; log Enphase homeowner account, approve, paste displayed code back R. Credentials tokens stored restrictive permissions tools::R_user_dir("enphaseR", "config") (override ENPHASER_CONFIG_DIR environment variable config_dir argument).
code: library(enphaseR)  enphase_authorize(   client_id = "your-client-id",   client_secret = "your-client-secret",   api_key = "your-api-key" )

title: Access Solar Production Data from the Enphase Enlighten Cloud API
text: Results plain tibbles — save however like (readr::write_csv(), saveRDS(), parquet, database, …). Expect totals differ Enlighten app around 1%. Enphase records production two ways: site’s revenue-grade production meter measures, microinverters report. disagree slightly measure different points system (one day author’s system: 59.21 vs 59.76 kWh, 0.9% gap). enphase_production() returns meter values; Enlighten app/website may display either source depending system’s configuration. Neither number wrong — just don’t surprised don’t match watt-hour. automate daily fetch cron Windows Task Scheduler — setup makes re-authentication unnecessary — see vignette: vignette("enphaseR").
code: # Find your system_id (most homeowners have exactly one system) enphase_systems()  # Yesterday's production, one row per 15-minute interval prod <- enphase_production(system_id = 1234567) prod #> # A tibble: 96 x 3 #>   date       end_at              wh_del #>   <date>     <dttm>               <dbl> #> 1 2026-07-17 2026-07-17 00:15:00      0 #> ...  # Backfill a date range (one API request per day, rate-limit aware) enphase_production(1234567, start_date = "2026-06-01", end_date = "2026-06-30")  # Current status and lifetime production enphase_summary(1234567)

title: Access Solar Production Data from the Enphase Enlighten Cloud API
text: Consumption, battery, endpoints aren’t covered yet; ’d slot request machinery ’s demand.

what: enphaseR: Access Solar Production Data from the Enphase Enlighten Cloud API — enphaseR-package
title: enphaseR: Access Solar Production Data from the Enphase Enlighten Cloud API — enphaseR-package
text: Fetch solar energy production data Enphase system official Enphase Enlighten cloud API (v4). Handles OAuth2 authorization flow one-time interactive browser step, keeps access refresh tokens perpetually fresh rotating every request, scheduled job never needs manual re-authentication. Returns tidy tibbles 15-minute production intervals, system lists, system summaries. Code mostly re-written Claude earlier scripts Sharon wrote.

title: enphaseR: Access Solar Production Data from the Enphase Enlighten Cloud API — enphaseR-package
text: Maintainer: Sharon Machlis smachlis@gmail.com

what: Authorize enphaseR with your Enphase developer application — enphase_authorize
title: Authorize enphaseR with your Enphase developer application — enphase_authorize
text: One-time interactive setup. Opens Enphase authorization page browser, log Enphase homeowner (Enlighten) account approve access; Enphase displays authorization code paste back R console. code exchanged access token (valid day) refresh token (valid month), saved – along application credentials – enphaseR config directory restrictive file permissions.

title: Authorize enphaseR with your Enphase developer application — enphase_authorize
code: enphase_authorize(   client_id = NULL,   client_secret = NULL,   api_key = NULL,   config_dir = NULL )

title: Authorize enphaseR with your Enphase developer application — enphase_authorize
text: client_id, client_secret, api_key Credentials application's page https://developer-v4.enphase.com (free "Watt" plan fine). application must granted access "System Details" "Site Level Production Monitoring" create – application missing category gets 401 endpoints even though credentials valid. Required first time; afterward default saved values. config_dir Directory credential token files. Defaults ENPHASER_CONFIG_DIR environment variable set, otherwise tools::R_user_dir("enphaseR", "config").

title: Authorize enphaseR with your Enphase developer application — enphase_authorize
text: TRUE, invisibly, success.

title: Authorize enphaseR with your Enphase developer application — enphase_authorize
text: one call, never need authorize browser long tokens get refreshed least monthly. Every enphaseR data function refreshes rotates token pair calling API, scheduled job runs least month (daily typical) keeps authentication alive indefinitely. See vignette("enphaseR") set . refresh token ever lapses (example, machine month), data functions fail message telling re-run function. re-run can omit credential arguments – saved ones reused – recovery single -argument call.

title: Authorize enphaseR with your Enphase developer application — enphase_authorize
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # First-time setup: enphase_authorize(   client_id = "your-client-id",   client_secret = "your-client-secret",   api_key = "your-api-key" )  # Re-authorizing after a lapsed refresh token: enphase_authorize() } # }

what: Fetch 15-minute solar production intervals for one or more days — enphase_production
title: Fetch 15-minute solar production intervals for one or more days — enphase_production
text: Retrieves production-meter telemetry Enphase API, one request per day range, returns intervals single tidy tibble. API serves past dates, missed days can backfilled widening range.

title: Fetch 15-minute solar production intervals for one or more days — enphase_production
code: enphase_production(   system_id,   start_date = Sys.Date() - 1,   end_date = start_date,   tz = "America/New_York",   config_dir = NULL )

title: Fetch 15-minute solar production intervals for one or more days — enphase_production
text: system_id system's numeric ID, enphase_systems(). start_date, end_date date range fetch (inclusive). Anything .Date() understands. default yesterday. tz Time zone system's location, used define day's midnight boundary display interval times. config_dir Directory credential token files. Defaults ENPHASER_CONFIG_DIR environment variable set, otherwise tools::R_user_dir("enphaseR", "config").

title: Fetch 15-minute solar production intervals for one or more days — enphase_production
text: tibble one row per 15-minute interval: date, end_at (interval's end time POSIXct tz), wh_del (watt-hours delivered). Saving – e.g. readr::write_csv() saveRDS() result.

title: Fetch 15-minute solar production intervals for one or more days — enphase_production
text: full day 96 fifteen-minute intervals. API returns fewer – typically Enphase finished reporting recent day – function keeps got warns, can re-fetch day later. Values come site's revenue-grade production meter. Enphase also records production reported microinverters , typically differs around 1% two measure different points system. Enlighten app/website may display either source, expect small discrepancies totals app. free "Watt" developer plan allows 1,000 requests per month 10 per minute. One request made per day range; ranges longer 10 days function paces stay per-minute limit, rate-limited responses retried automatically.

title: Fetch 15-minute solar production intervals for one or more days — enphase_production
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Yesterday's production: enphase_production(system_id = 1234567)  # Backfill a month: enphase_production(1234567, start_date = "2026-06-01",                    end_date = "2026-06-30") } # }

what: Refresh and rotate the saved Enphase tokens — enphase_refresh_tokens
title: Refresh and rotate the saved Enphase tokens — enphase_refresh_tokens
text: Exchanges saved refresh token new access/refresh token pair saves rotated pair back disk. Access tokens last day refresh tokens month, refreshing every run – enphaseR data functions automatically – keeps perpetually fresh. need call directly want scheduled job keep tokens alive without fetching data.

title: Refresh and rotate the saved Enphase tokens — enphase_refresh_tokens
text: config_dir Directory credential token files. Defaults ENPHASER_CONFIG_DIR environment variable set, otherwise tools::R_user_dir("enphaseR", "config").

title: Refresh and rotate the saved Enphase tokens — enphase_refresh_tokens
text: Invisibly, list elements access_token api_key, suitable authenticating API requests.

title: Refresh and rotate the saved Enphase tokens — enphase_refresh_tokens
